rows.notes: Very clear centered year heading, underlined. No amounts present; unambiguous.
Clear title directly under the year. No monetary values in this line.

Description legible. Pounds '66' aligns with left-most of the three amount rules. Shillings '13' and pence '6' are clear; a small loop after the pence resembles a three‑farthing mark (interpreted as 0.75). Column alignment consistent with following rows.

Abbreviated description 'Do Do' (ditto). Pounds appear as a three-digit number '373' aligned in the pounds column. Shillings '8' clear. Pence '6' followed by a loop similar to above (taken as three‑farthings, 0.75). Slight ink smudge on the first digit of the pounds so confidence reduced.
Clear description. Pounds read as '234' (three-digit). Shillings '8' and pence '6' are legible; no fractional mark after the pence. Vertical alignment with other amounts supports reading.
Short description 'Bushey Divd.' is readable. Pounds '7' small but clearly in pounds column. Shillings '4' and pence '6' visible though slightly cramped; no fraction observed.
Section label introducing numbered sub-items. No amount directly on this line; heading is legible.

The line shows the item '1' under Masters Composition. To the right a combined amount reads as '22 3 0' (pounds 22, shillings 3, pence 0). Some ink crowding from adjacent explanatory notes (Domus/local rate) makes the description abbreviated; amount alignment clear but description uncertain.
Second numbered Masters Composition item. Pounds read '11' (small), shillings '8' and pence '6' align in their columns. Ink from adjoining script overlaps slightly, reducing confidence.
Line explicitly marked '3. Nil' (i.e. nothing to account for). No monetary figures present and the 'Nil' is clear.

Description legible as '3 sets of Rooms St Helens'. Amount reads as a small '3' in pounds column, with '0.0' for shillings and pence. Pound figure is small and could be a stray mark but aligns with column, so accepted.
Surname 'Dickerson' (spelled with an o) followed by 'Balance of Rent' readable. Amount clearly '43 0 0' in the amount columns, well aligned and free of smudge.

rows.description: Int on £57.10 at 3 per ct 1:14:4 by Acton
rows.notes: Interest line for £57.10 at 3% — the computed interest '1 14 4' is clearly written in the amount columns. Minor stylistic flourish on colon but digits are unambiguous.
Text reads 'Repayment of Capitale' (final 'e' present in ms). Amount '2 10 0' is clear and correctly aligned. No fraction mark.

rows.description: Int on £100 at 4 per cent Augt 18-1880 by Do
rows.notes: Interest on £100 line. Amount '4 0 0' very clear and in correct columns. Date and reference 'by Do' legible.

rows.description: Int on £150 at Do to April 1.1880 by Do
rows.notes: Interest on £150 line. Amount '6 0 0' is clearly written and aligned; legible reference to the date and 'by Do'.

Description reads as 'Late Readers Prize' (handwriting slightly compressed). Amount shows '13 2 10'; the '2' shillings and '10' pence are readable though pence has slight ink blotting, giving moderate confidence.

rows.description: Int for one yr. to Domus for £500 by Bursar
rows.notes: Interest for one year line. Pounds '15' clear; shillings and pence appear as '0 0'. 'Bursar' is legible as the payer. Good vertical alignment.

Description truncated/abbreviated on the line ('mostly &c of' — trailing words faint). Amount read as '14 4 3' but the first digit of pounds is slightly unclear and could be '114' though alignment suggests a two‑digit value. Because of that ambiguity confidence is lowered.
Entry 'Boase Register' clear. Amount '10 0 0' is neatly written and unambiguous.
Marginal date 'Jul 14' marking a new sub-section; no amount on this line. Date is legible though slightly cramped in margin.

Clear phrase 'Cash in Bank Shortlands' (shortened to 'Shortlands' or 'Shortlands' — interpreted as Shortlands). Amount '122.15.0' written with dots but aligns perfectly with £ s d columns; very clear.
Continuation 'Do Do Richards' (i.e. Cash in Bank — Richards). Amount '93 3 4' clearly written and unambiguous.
Third bank entry (name reads 'Trukell' or similar; handwriting makes the surname slightly uncertain but the amounts '46 19 8' are clear and properly aligned).

rows.description: Stock of the three funds sold
rows.notes: Long amount '6067 17 4' clearly written in pounds (four digits), shillings and pence. Description 'Stock of the three funds sold' readable though slightly abbreviated; alignment is unambiguous.
Description 'Canal Shares sold' clear. Amount '347 4 0' unambiguous and aligned in columns.

A multi-digit total appears to the right of the Canal Shares line reading as '2277 19 4' (the image shows '22/7/19/4' style). There is ambiguity in whether this is '2277' or '22 7' with extra marks; chosen reading '2277 19s 4d' because of column alignment with other totals. Confidence lowered due to unusual notation and slight ink smearing.
Marginal date 'July 1' clearly present, marking subsequent items. No monetary amount on this line.

Description reads as a college name 'Selborne (or Selborne-like) College for Witham Road' — the first word is slightly uncertain but context suggests 'Selborne'. Amount '10 0 0' is clearly written. Reduced confidence due to the proper‑name uncertainty.
Line reads 'Hammond. Bursar allowed to Soden' (surname 'Soden' faint but plausible). Amount '25 0 0' clear. Slight uncertainty in the recipient name lowers confidence.
Second instance of 'Boase Register' further down the page. Amount clearly '2 0 0'. Description and amount both legible.
Name 'Clutterbuck' is quite legible followed by 'for Witham Road'. Amount '10 0 0' is clear and aligned.

Final bottom-right grand total is visible as '3390 0 4' with a small fractional loop after the pence interpreted as a half‑penny (0.5). The leading pounds '3390' and the 4d are readable though the fractional mark is small and slightly ambiguous, so confidence is moderate.
